name: feat-ESE-15
title: Multi-view setups
description:
Configure the view system to support multiple cameras rendering to different surfaces.

Three modes:
1. **Split views** — multiple cameras render side-by-side directly to browser viewport regions (split-screen gaming). When child views fully cover the parent, they render directly to screen — no intermediate textures.
2. **Nested views** — a camera renders to an offscreen texture (RenderTarget), displayed on a RenderQuad node in another scene (TV in a room, security camera feed).
3. **Hall of mirrors** — recursive rendering with depth limit. Camera catches a quad showing its own output. Previous pass or previous frame used to break cycles.

All cameras render to a surface: either a RenderTarget (texture) or a ScreenRegion (viewport slice). The browser window is just the final surface.

New RenderView abstraction pairs camera + output surface. Views form a dependency graph, rendered in topological order (leaves first).

New RenderQuad scene node type displays a named render target texture on a quad in 3D space.

Architecture: docs/multi-view-architecture.md
acceptanceCriteria:
- Split screen: two cameras rendering to left/right halves of the browser window via viewport scissoring
- Nested view: a camera renders to a texture displayed on a RenderQuad in another scene
- Hall of mirrors: recursive render with configurable depth limit
- RenderView abstraction supports both ScreenRegion and RenderTarget outputs
- RenderQuad scene node type displays named render target textures
- Renderer interface extended with createRenderTarget, setRenderTarget, setViewport, setMaterialTexture
- Views rendered in dependency order (topological sort)
- Split screen optimization: direct-to-screen when children fully cover parent
demoDeliverable: Visual demo showing: (1) split-screen with two cameras viewing the same scene from different angles, (2) a scene containing a TV quad displaying a live feed from another camera/scene
testingScenarios:
- Create two RenderViews with ScreenRegion outputs (split screen), verify both render independently
- Create a RenderView with RenderTarget output, verify texture is produced
- Create a RenderQuad node referencing the texture, verify it displays in the parent scene
- Verify dependency ordering: nested view camera renders before parent camera
- Verify hall-of-mirrors with recursionDepth=2 produces visible recursion
- Verify split screen optimization: no intermediate texture when children cover parent
testingNotes: Unit tests for dependency graph and topological sort. Integration tests for split screen viewport setup. Visual tests for nested view and hall of mirrors.
stakeholderUnderstanding: Building out the view system to support a range of rendering scenarios — from GUI-facing multi-window setups (split screen) to complex in-scene compositions (TV in a room, hall of mirrors, split screen on a TV). Validated by creating each scenario and demoing.
comments: Architecture doc: docs/multi-view-architecture.md. This is a large ticket (size 13) — likely needs subtasks for split screen, nested views, and hall of mirrors individually.
